diagrams.title: OSI Model Layer Structure
diagrams.description: A vertical diagram showing 7 layers from top (Application) to bottom (Physical) with arrows showing data flow direction and arrows showing encapsulation/decapsulation process
elements: Application Layer (7) - Network services to users
Presentation Layer (6) - Data formatting and encryption
Session Layer (5) - Session management
Transport Layer (4) - End-to-end connection
Network Layer (3) - Routing and logical addressing
Data Link Layer (2) - Node-to-node transfer, MAC
Physical Layer (1) - Physical transmission of bits
Arrows showing downward encapsulation and upward decapsulation
diagrams.purpose: Understanding layer hierarchy and data flow direction through the model

diagrams.title: OSI Encapsulation Process
diagrams.description: A horizontal diagram showing how data is transformed at each layer as it moves from sender (left) to receiver (right) across the network
elements: Application Data → Transport Segment → Network Packet → Data Link Frame → Physical Bits
Headers added at each layer: Application/Presentation/Session header, TCP/UDP header, IP header, MAC header/trailer
PDU names at each layer clearly labeled
Shows the 'wrapper' concept where each layer encapsulates the previous layer's data
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how data is transformed and wrapped at each layer during transmission

diagrams.title: OSI Layer Communication Flow
diagrams.description: A diagram showing two end systems communicating through a network, with each system showing all 7 layers, and intermediate devices (router, switch) showing only relevant layers
elements: Sender system with all 7 layers
Network with Router (Layer 3) and Switch (Layer 2) between sender and receiver
Receiver system with all 7 layers
Dashed lines showing logical communication between same layers on sender and receiver
Solid lines showing actual physical communication through the network
Router shown at Network Layer only
Switch shown at Data Link Layer only
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how different devices operate at different OSI layers during actual communication

diagrams.title: OSI vs TCP/IP Model Comparison
diagrams.description: A side-by-side comparison chart showing the 7-layer OSI model mapped to the 4-layer TCP/IP model with examples of protocols at each layer
elements: OSI 7 layers: Physical, Data Link, Network, Transport, Session, Presentation, Application
TCP/IP 4 layers: Network Access, Internet, Transport, Application
Mapping: OSI Physical+Data Link → TCP/IP Network Access; OSI Network → TCP/IP Internet; OSI Transport → TCP/IP Transport; OSI Session+Presentation+Application → TCP/IP Application
Protocol examples for each layer in both models
diagrams.purpose: Understanding relationship between theoretical OSI and practical TCP/IP models
